title: OpenSkyData Route Trace
description: An observed flight path of a drone or group of drones during an attack event. Reconstructed from ground observer reports, air force tracking, and OSINT. These are approximate routes through named locations, not precise GPS tracks.

id.description: Unique route ID. Format: {event_id}_R{seq}. e.g. UA_20250907_001_R01

event_id.description: Links to the parent attack event

drone_count.description: Number of drones on this route if distinguishable. Often 1, sometimes a group. Null if unknown.

drone_type.description: Type of drone if identifiable. e.g. 'shahed_136', 'geran_3', 'gerbera_decoy'. Null if unknown.

outcome.description: What happened to this drone/group. Null if unknown.

waypoints.description: Ordered sequence of locations the drone was observed passing through or near. Minimum 2 (origin and last known position).

name.description: Location name. Use oblast/region for broad tracking, city for precise. e.g. 'Mykolaiv', 'Kirovohrad Oblast', 'Kyiv'

lat.description: Latitude. Can be geocoded from name if not provided directly.

lon.description: Longitude. Can be geocoded from name if not provided directly.

type.description: What this waypoint represents in the route

time_approx.description: Approximate time at this waypoint (HH:MM UTC) if known

approach_direction.description: Overall approach direction toward target. Null if unclear or circuitous route.

route_type.description: Route behaviour pattern. 'direct'=roughly straight line, 'circuitous'=deliberate detour through multiple oblasts, 'looping'=drone observed doubling back.

estimated_distance_km.description: Approximate total route distance in km if calculable from waypoints.

notes.description: Notable observations. e.g. 'Drone circled Khmelnytskyi twice before heading to Ternopil', 'Intercepted by Sting over Vinnytsia'
